samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with the Java 21 migration.
samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21; the application will fail to compile or run due to incompatible bytecode and API changes in the Spring framework.

samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.general_idea: Set the Java version property to 21 to configure the build for the target JDK.
samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this property, the build may default to an older Java version or fail to configure plugins correctly for Java 21 features.

samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the maven-compiler-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the maven-compiler-plugin may not support compiling Java 21 source code.

samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace source/target compiler settings with the release flag for Java 21 compilation.
samples/SimpleSpringApp/pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' flags are deprecated in favor of 'release' for modern Java versions; using them with Java 21 may result in compilation errors or incorrect bytecode generation.
